Callout that displays information about a selected annotation near the
annotation.

Style property for the CalloutNativeComponent.

Use at your own risk.

props.description: Style property for the Animated.View wrapper, apply animations to this

props.name: titleStyle

props.name: initialViewState
props.description: Default view settings applied on camera

props.name: minZoom
props.description: Minimum zoom level of the map

props.name: maxZoom
props.description: Maximum zoom level of the map

props.name: maxBounds
props.description: Restrict map panning so that the center is within these bounds

props.name: trackUserLocation
props.description:
The mode used to track the user location on the map:

- undefined: The user's location is not tracked
- "default": Centers the user's location
- "heading": Centers the user's location and uses the compass for bearing
- "course": Centers the user's location and uses the direction of travel for
  bearing

@defaultValue undefined

props.name: onTrackUserLocationChange
props.description:
Triggered when `trackUserLocation` changes
*signature:*`(event:NativeSyntheticEvent) => void`

props.name: ref
props.description: Ref to access Camera methods.

GeoJSONSource.description:
GeoJSONSource is a map content source that supplies GeoJSON to be shown on
the map. The data may be provided as an url or a GeoJSON object.

props.name: id
props.description: A string that uniquely identifies the source.

props.name: data
props.description:
Can be provided as one of:
- An HTTP(S) URL, absolute file URL, or local file URL relative to the current
  application’s resource bundle
- Any valid GeoJSON object

props.name: cluster
props.description: Enables clustering on the source

props.name: clusterRadius
props.description:
Specifies the radius of each cluster if clustering is enabled. A value of 512
produces a radius equal to the width of a tile. The default value is 50.

props.name: clusterMinPoints
props.description:
Specifies minimum number of points to form a cluster if clustering is
enabled. The default value is 2.

props.name: clusterMaxZoom
props.description:
Specifies the maximum zoom level at which to cluster points if clustering is
enabled. Defaults to one zoom level less than the value of maxzoom so that,
at the maximum zoom, the data is not clustered.

props.name: clusterProperties
props.description:
Specifies custom properties on the generated clusters if clustering is
enabled, aggregating values from clustered points.

Has the form `{ "property_name": [operator, map_expression]}` , where
`operator` is a custom reduce expression that references a special
`["accumulated"]` value - it accumulates the property value from
clusters/points the cluster contains `map_expression` produces the value of a
single point

@example `{ "resultingSum": [["+", ["accumulated"], ["get", "resultingSum"]], ["get", "scalerank"]] }`

props.name: maxzoom
props.description:
Specifies the maximum zoom level at which to create vector tiles. A greater
value produces greater detail at high zoom levels. The default value is 18.

props.name: buffer
props.description:
Specifies the size of the tile buffer on each side. A value of 0 produces no
buffer. A value of 512 produces a buffer as wide as the tile itself. Larger
values produce fewer rendering artifacts near tile edges and slower
performance. The default value is 128.

props.name: tolerance
lineMetrics
props.description:
Whether to calculate line distance metrics. This is required for line layers
that specify lineGradient values. The default value is false.

props.name: children
props.description: FIX ME NO DESCRIPTION

props.name: ref
props.description: Ref to access GeoJSONSource methods.

GeoJSONSource.name: GeoJSONSource

ImageSource.description:
ImageSource is a content source that is used for a georeferenced raster image
to be shown on the map. The georeferenced image scales and rotates as the
user zooms and rotates the map

props.name: id
props.description: A string that uniquely identifies the source.

props.name: url
props.description:
An HTTP(S) URL, absolute file URL, or local file URL to the source image.
Animated GIFs are not supported.

props.name: coordinates
props.description:
The top left, top right, bottom right, and bottom left coordinates for the
image.

props.name: children
props.description: FIX ME NO DESCRIPTION

ImageSource.name: ImageSource

Images.description:
Images defines the images used in Symbol layers.

Use this component to add images to the map style that can be referenced by
symbol layers using the `iconImage` property.

props.name: images
props.description:
Specifies the images in key-value pairs required for the style. Keys are
names used in style expressions (e.g., "customIcon").

Values provide a `source`, which can be one of the following types:
- A string URL: `"https://example.com/icon.png"`
- A native asset name: `"pin"` (from xcassets on iOS or drawable on Android)
- A require/import: `require('./icon.png')`

If your image supports SDF, you can set the `sdf` property to true:
`{ source: require('./sdf-icon.png'), sdf: true }`

@example
```tsx
<Images
  images={{
    remote: "https://example.com/marker.png",
    require: require("./marker.png"),
    native: "marker",
    sdf: { source: require("./sdf-marker.png"), sdf: true },
  }}
/>;
```

props.name: onImageMissing

Images.name: Images

Represents bounds in geographic coordinatesUses order of south-west and north-east corners in flat style per GeoJSON
RFC.

props.name: style
props.description:
Style for wrapping React Native View

@defaultValue { flex: 1 }

props.name: mapStyle
props.description:
Maplibre style - either a URL or a Style JSON.

@see {@link https://maplibre.org/maplibre-style-spec/}

props.name: light
contentInset
props.description:
The distance from the edges of the map view's frame to the edges of the map
view's logical viewport.

props.name: preferredFramesPerSecond
props.description:
iOS: The preferred frame rate at which the map view is rendered. The default
value for this property is MLNMapViewPreferredFramesPerSecondDefault, which
will adaptively set the preferred frame rate based on the capability of the
user’s device to maintain a smooth experience. This property can be set to
arbitrary integer values.

Android: The maximum frame rate at which the map view is rendered, but it
can't excess the ability of device hardware. This property can be set to
arbitrary integer values.

props.description:
Toggle pan interaction of the map

@defaultValue true

doubleTapZoom

@defaultValue true

@defaultValue true

logo

compass

Toggle the compass from hiding when facing north

@defaultValue true

androidView
onPress
props.description:
Called when a user presses the map

If the event bubbles up from a child `Source` with an `onPress` handler the
`features` will be included. The event will emit on `Map` and `Source` . To
prevent this use `event.stopPropagation()` in the `Source` handler.
*signature:*`(event:union) => void`

Called when a user long presses the map

props.name: onDidFinishLoadingMap

props.name: onWillStartRenderingMap

*signature:*`(event:NativeSyntheticEvent) => void`

props.name: onDidFinishRenderingMap

Marker.description:
Marker allows you to place an interactive React Native View on the map.

If you have static view consider using ViewAnnotation or SymbolLayer for
better performance.

Implemented through:
- Android: Native Views placed on the map projection
- iOS:
  [MLNPointAnnotation](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native/ios/latest/documentation/maplibre/mlnpointannotation/)

props.name: id
props.description: A string that uniquely identifies the marker.

props.name: lngLat
props.description:
The center point (specified as a map coordinate) of the marker. See also
#anchor.

props.name: anchor
offset
selected
props.description:
Manually selects/deselects the marker.

@platform iOS

props.name: onPress
props.description:
This callback is fired when the marker is pressed.
*signature:*`(event:NativeSyntheticEvent) => void`

props.name: children
props.description: Expects one child - can be a View with multiple elements.

props.name: ref
props.description: Ref to access Marker methods.

Marker.name: Marker

NetworkManager.name: NetworkManager
NetworkManager.description:
NetworkManager provides methods for managing and controlling network
connectivity.

OfflineManager.name: OfflineManager
OfflineManager.description:
OfflineManager implements a singleton (shared object) that manages offline
packs. All of this class's instance methods are asynchronous, reflecting the
fact that offline resources are stored in a database. The shared object
maintains a canonical collection of offline packs.

methods.name: createPack
methods.description:
Creates and registers an offline pack that downloads the resources needed to
use the given region offline.

params.name: options
params.description:
Create options for offline pack that specifies zoom levels, style url, and
the region to download.

params.name: progressListener
params.description:
Callback that listens for status events while downloading the offline
resource.

params.name: errorListener
params.description:
Callback that listens for status events while downloading the offline
resource.

returns.description: The created offline pack with its generated ID.

methods.name: invalidatePack
methods.description:
Invalidates the specified offline pack. This method checks that the tiles in
the specified offline pack match those from the server. Local tiles that do
not match the latest version on the server are updated.This is more efficient than deleting the offline pack and downloading it
again. If the data stored locally matches that on the server, new data will
not be downloaded.

methods.name: deletePack
methods.description:
Unregisters the given OfflinePack and allows resources that are no longer
required by any remaining packs to be potentially freed.

methods.name: invalidateAmbientCache
methods.description:
Forces a revalidation of the tiles in the ambient cache and downloads a fresh
version of the tiles from the tile server. This is the recommend method for
clearing the cache. This is the most efficient method because tiles in the
ambient cache are re-downloaded to remove outdated data from a device. It
does not erase resources from the ambient cache or delete the database, which
can be computationally expensive operations that may carry unintended side
effects.

methods.name: clearAmbientCache
methods.description:
Erases resources from the ambient cache. This method clears the cache and
decreases the amount of space that map resources take up on the device.

methods.name: setMaximumAmbientCacheSize
methods.description:
Sets the maximum size of the ambient cache in bytes. Disables the ambient
cache if set to 0. This method may be computationally expensive because it
will erase resources from the ambient cache if its size is decreased.

methods.name: resetDatabase
methods.description:
Deletes the existing database, which includes both the ambient cache and
offline packs, then reinitializes it.

methods.name: getPacks
methods.description: Retrieves all the current offline packs that are stored in the database.

methods.name: getPack
methods.description: Retrieves an offline pack that is stored in the database by ID.

methods.name: mergeOfflineRegions
methods.description: Sideloads offline db

params.name: path
params.description: Path to offline tile db on file system.

methods.name: setTileCountLimit
methods.description:
Sets the maximum number of tiles that may be downloaded and stored on the
current device. Consult the Terms of Service for your map tile host before
changing this value.

methods.name: setProgressEventThrottle
methods.description:
Sets the period at which download status events will be sent over the React
Native bridge. The default is 500ms.

methods.name: addListener
methods.description:
Subscribe to download status/error events for the requested offline pack.
Note that createPack calls this internally if listeners are provided.

params.name: progressListener
params.description:
Callback that listens for status events while downloading the offline
resource.

params.name: errorListener
params.description:
Callback that listens for status events while downloading the offline
resource.

methods.name: removeListener
methods.description:
Unsubscribes any listeners associated with the offline pack. Should be called
when the component unmounts.

RasterSource.description:
RasterSource is a map content source that supplies raster image tiles to be
shown on the map. The location of and metadata about the tiles are defined
either by an option dictionary or by an external file that conforms to the
TileJSON specification.

props.name: id
props.description: A string that uniquely identifies the source.

props.name: url
props.description:
A URL to a TileJSON configuration file describing the source's contents and
other metadata.

props.name: tiles
minzoom
props.description:
An unsigned integer that specifies the minimum zoom level at which to display
tiles from the source. The value should be between 0 and 22, inclusive, and
less than maxzoom, if specified. The default value for this option is 0.

props.name: maxzoom
props.description:
An unsigned integer that specifies the maximum zoom level at which to display
tiles from the source. The value should be between 0 and 22, inclusive, and
less than minzoom, if specified. The default value for this option is 22.

props.name: tileSize
props.description:
Size of the map tiles.

@defaultValue 512

props.name: scheme
props.description:
Influences the y direction of the tile coordinates. (tms inverts y-axis)

@defaultValue "xyz"

props.name: attribution
props.description:
An HTML or literal text string defining the buttons to be displayed in an
action sheet when the source is part of a map view's style and the map view's
attribution button is pressed.

props.name: children
props.description: FIX ME NO DESCRIPTION

RasterSource.name: RasterSource

StaticMapManager.name: StaticMapManager
StaticMapManager.description: The StaticMapManager creates static images of a map.

methods.name: createImage
methods.description: Creates a static image of a map. Images are always in PNG format.

TransformRequestManager.name: TransformRequestManager
TransformRequestManager.description:
TransformRequestManager provides methods for managing HTTP requests made by
MapLibre.Transformations are possible in three ways:Transforms are applied in this order. The  conditions are applied to
possibly already transformed URLs.To gain insight into which transforms are applied set the log level to
 via :

methods.name: addUrlTransform
methods.description:
Adds or updates a URL transform identified by .Transforms execute in insertion order. Therefore  and  regexes
are matched against possibly already modified URL by previous transforms.Re-adding an existing  updates the transform , preserving its
position in the pipeline. This makes it safe to refresh tokens or swap
domains without disrupting the order of other transforms.URL transforms are applied before  and .

params.name: options
params.description:
The transform. Set  to a stable string to enable
in-place updates; if omitted an id is auto-generated and returned.

returns.description: The id of the transform (the value of  when provided, otherwise the auto-generated one). Pass it to  to remove it later.

methods.name: removeUrlTransform
methods.description:
Removes the URL transform with the given  . No-op if the id is not
registered.

methods.name: clearUrlTransforms
methods.description: Removes all registered URL transforms

methods.name: addUrlSearchParam
methods.description:
Adds or updates a URL query parameter identified by  that will be
appended to all matching map resource requests. Re-adding an existing 
updates the param in-place.

params.name: options
params.description:
The options. Set  to a stable string to enable
in-place updates; if omitted an id is auto-generated and returned.

returns.description: The id of the options. Pass it to  to remove it later.

methods.name: removeUrlSearchParam
methods.description: Removes a previously added URL query parameter by its .

methods.name: addHeader
methods.description:
Adds or updates an HTTP header identified by  that will be sent with all
matching map resource requests. Re-adding an existing  updates the header
in-place.

params.name: options
params.description:
The options. Set  to a stable string to enable
in-place updates; if omitted an id is auto-generated and returned.

examples:
Add header to all requests
```
TransformRequestManager.addHeader({ name: "Authorization", value: "Bearer token123" });
```

returns.description: The id of the options. Pass it to  to remove it later.

methods.name: clearUrlSearchParams
methods.description: Removes all registered URL search params.

methods.name: removeHeader
methods.description: Removes a previously added HTTP header by its .

methods.name: clearHeaders
methods.description: Removes all registered HTTP headers.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Removes all registered URL transforms, URL search params and HTTP headers.

props.name: children
props.description:
Children to render inside the UserLocation Annotation, e.g. CircleLayer,
SymbolLayer

props.name: animated
props.description: Whether the UserLocation Annotation is animated between updates

props.name: accuracy
props.description: Render a circle which indicates the accuracy of the location

props.name: heading
props.description:
Render an arrow which indicates direction the device is pointing relative to
north

props.name: minDisplacement
props.description: Minimum delta in meters for location updates

props.name: onPress
props.description:
Event triggered on pressing the UserLocation Annotation
*signature:*`() => void`

VectorSource.description:
VectorSource is a map content source that supplies tiled vector data in
Mapbox Vector Tile format to be shown on the map. The location of and
metadata about the tiles are defined either by an option dictionary or by an
external file that conforms to the TileJSON specification.

props.name: id
props.description: A string that uniquely identifies the source.

props.name: url
props.description:
A URL to a TileJSON configuration file describing the source’s contents and
other metadata.

props.name: tiles
props.description:
An array of tile URL templates. If multiple endpoints are specified, clients
may use any combination of endpoints. Example:
https://example.com/vector-tiles/{z}/{x}/{y}.pbf

props.name: minzoom
props.description:
An unsigned integer that specifies the minimum zoom level at which to display
tiles from the source. The value should be between 0 and 22, inclusive, and
less than maxzoom, if specified. The default value for this option is 0.

props.name: maxzoom
props.description:
An unsigned integer that specifies the maximum zoom level at which to display
tiles from the source. The value should be between 0 and 22, inclusive, and
less than minzoom, if specified. The default value for this option is 22.

props.name: scheme
props.description:
Influences the y direction of the tile coordinates. (tms inverts y-axis)

@defaultValue "xyz"

props.name: attribution
props.description:
An HTML or literal text string defining the buttons to be displayed in an
action sheet when the source is part of a map view’s style and the map view’s
attribution button is pressed.

props.name: children
props.description: FIX ME NO DESCRIPTION

props.name: ref
props.description: Ref to access VectorSource methods.

VectorSource.name: VectorSource

ViewAnnotation.description:
ViewAnnotation represents a one-dimensional shape located at a single
geographical coordinate.

Consider using GeoJSONSource and SymbolLayer instead, if you have many
points, and you have static images, they'll offer much better performance.

If you need interactive views please use Marker, as with ViewAnnotation on
Android child views are rendered onto a bitmap for better performance.

props.name: id
props.description:
A string that uniquely identifies the annotation. If not provided, a unique
ID will be generated automatically.

props.name: title
props.description:
The string containing the annotation's title. Note this is required to be set
if you want to see a callout appear on iOS.

props.name: snippet
props.description:
The string containing the annotation's snippet(subtitle). Not displayed in
the default callout.

props.name: selected
props.description: Manually selects/deselects annotation

props.name: draggable
props.description:
Enable or disable dragging.

@defaultValue false

props.name: lngLat
props.description: The center point (specified as a map coordinate) of the annotation.

props.name: anchor
offset
onPress
props.description:
This callback is fired when the annotation is pressed.
*signature:*`(event:NativeSyntheticEvent) => void`

props.name: onSelect
props.description:
This callback is fired once this annotation is selected.
*signature:*`(event:NativeSyntheticEvent) => void`

props.name: onDeselect
props.description:
This callback is fired once this annotation is deselected.
*signature:*`(event:NativeSyntheticEvent) => void`

props.name: onDragStart
props.description:
This callback is fired once this annotation has started being dragged.
*signature:*`(event:NativeSyntheticEvent) => void`

props.name: onDragEnd
props.description:
This callback is fired once this annotation has stopped being dragged.
*signature:*`(event:NativeSyntheticEvent) => void`

props.name: onDrag
props.description:
This callback is fired while this annotation is being dragged.
*signature:*`(event:NativeSyntheticEvent) => void`

props.name: style
props.description: FIX ME NO DESCRIPTION

props.name: children
props.description: Expects one child, and an optional callout can be added as well

props.name: ref
props.description: Ref to access ViewAnnotation methods.

ViewAnnotation.name: ViewAnnotation
